observations.summary: implement-story applied a CSS utility to 3 of 4 scrollable panels (missing the full-width artifact list pane at line 123); the gap was caught by review-nfrs as a Medium finding but only documented, not fixed, and the story was committed as done with the gap unresolved
observations.evidence: NFR assessment _bmad-output/test-artifacts/nfr-assessment-5-4.md Finding 2 (lines 150-163): 'no-scrollbar not applied to full-width artifact list pane [artifacts/page.tsx:123] — Introduced by Story 5.4: Yes (AC-7 coverage gap) — Status: Open.' Story file review section line 459: '[NFR][Medium] no-scrollbar not applied to full-width artifact list pane [artifacts/page.tsx:123] — Introduced by Story 5.4 (AC-7 added no-scrollbar to line 93 but missed line 123).' deferred-work.md line 371: 'Scrollable panels missing .no-scrollbar — [artifacts/page.tsx:123] (full-width artifact list pane — noted as NFR finding in story but not previously transferred here).' In 5.4 the gap slipped through unit-tests and review-code, was caught by review-nfrs but only documented.

observations.summary: The withArtifacts E2E test fixture is broken (POST /api/internal/test/artifacts returns 500 due to unique constraint violations on [repoConnectionId, path]), causing e2e-tests to create skipped tests that review-tests then removes in the same run — a create-skip-remove cycle that has persisted across Stories 5.1, 5.2, and 5.4
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for e2e-tests: 'Pre-existing Issue Discovered — The withArtifacts fixture is broken — POST /api/internal/test/artifacts returns 500 due to unique constraint violations on [repoConnectionId, path]. This affects ALL tests using withArtifacts, including the existing Story 2.2 project-map.spec.ts. The AC-1 and AC-7 artifact pane tests are skipped with documented comments.' Journal step_end for review-tests: 'Story 5.4 E2E — 2 skipped tests removed: AC-1 describe.skip (withArtifacts fixture broken with no planned fix), AC-7 P1 test.skip (empty body).' Ledger entry 78 (Story 5.1): '6 tests remain skipped due to different pre-existing environment issues (Server Action mocking, artifact rendering, EventSource mock).' Ledger entry 87 (Story 5.2): 'AC-8 on /artifacts deferred — the page Server Component hangs in the E2E environment (GitHub API timeout with fake repo).' The fixture breakage is a distinct infrastructure issue from the auth.setup.ts seed-user issue (infra-e2e-env-config-missing, resolved in 5.1). runner-errors.jsonl has no entry for this run (5.4) — the fixture breakage is a test-environment issue, not a runner failure.

observations.summary: Prior hypothesized observation (ledger entry 90, Story 5.3 e2e-tests) — the hypothesis remains unconfirmable: no traces exist for any run (the traces directory does not exist), so the pointer cannot be followed; 5.4 had 0 halts so the pattern did not recur this run
observations.evidence: Ledger entry 90 (Story 5.3): 'e2e-tests recorded halts:1 with outcomeHistory [COMPLETE] and no question in the responseExcerpt — no runner-error and normal duration, so the cause cannot be determined without a trace. nextStep: When traces become available for this run, widen into traces/5.3-a1-20260712T095855/e2e-tests.json with --grep HALT or --grep QUESTION.' trace-view.mjs 5.3-a1-20260712T095855 e2e-tests: 'no trace for this step.' ls _bmad-output/pipeline/traces/: 'NO_TRACES_DIR' — the traces directory does not exist. Journal story_end for 5.4: all 12 steps status success, 0 halts. Trends haltsPerStory: 5.1:1, 5.3:2 — 5.4 not listed (0 halts).
observations.hypothesis: The 5.3 e2e-tests halt was either a classifier ambiguity (response was neither clearly COMPLETE nor QUESTION at some mid-stream point) or a genuine question posed and resolved before the final outcome — but without traces, the cause cannot be determined. The 5.1 validate-story phantom halt was confirmed in 5.3 as runner-timeout-triggered (ledger entry 86), but the 5.3 e2e-tests halt had no runner-error and normal duration, distinguishing it from the timeout pattern.
observations.nextStep: When trace recording is enabled and traces become available, widen into traces/5.3-a1-20260712T095855/e2e-tests.json with --grep 'HALT' or --grep 'QUESTION' to find the mid-stream point where the halt form fired; check whether a genuine question was posed or whether the halt was triggered by a classifier ambiguity. Until traces exist, this hypothesis cannot be confirmed or refuted.

amendments.prompt: run Create mode. Find NFR-specific issues only (e.g., missing select projections, take limits, timing tests, security headers). Document findings (severity + remediation) in the story's review section. For findings you determine were introduced by the current story and that have a straightforward remediation (e.g., a missing class or attribute the story should have applied to a location it touched), apply the fix directly rather than only documenting it; for findings that are pre-existing or require complex remediation, document them as before.
amendments.rationale: review-nfrs catches AC implementation gaps introduced by the current story but only documents them; applying straightforward fixes would prevent the gap from being committed as-is, as happened with the AC-7 no-scrollbar coverage gap at artifacts/page.tsx:123.
